Escaramuza Ameyali · A California Nonprofit

Riding for heritage.
Riding for the children.

Ameyali Foundation is a Gilroy-based nonprofit and escaramuza team, founded to support and uplift children with special needs and disabilities through tradition, performance, and community.

Donate See our story

What we are

Three pillars, one mission.

A women's equestrian tradition, a community of riders, and a new nonprofit committed to children who deserve more chances.

01

Heritage

An all-women's tradition since 2019.

Escaramuza is the only women's event in charrería, Mexican rodeo. Founded March 12, 2019, our team of eight rides side-saddle in a lienzo, performing synchronized maneuvers to music in traditional dresses and sombreros — honoring the adelitas of the Mexican Revolution.

02

Mission

Support for children with special needs.

Ameyali Foundation was incorporated in 2026 with one stated purpose: to provide support and uplift special needs children and children with other disabilities — through advocacy, community, and time with our horses.

03

Familia Charra

A sisterhood that shows up.

In this sport we become a familia charra — we help each other when in need, celebrate each other's wins, and lift each other through every challenge. We bring that same care to the children and causes we serve.

Our story

From a team to a foundation.

More than a team — a sisterhood built on tradition, respect, and love for our heritage.

Escaramuza Ameyali began on March 12, 2019, with a purpose: to teach our daughters the love and passion for Mexican tradition and culture. The sport teaches discipline, strength, respect, responsibility, intense teamwork, and horsemanship — and along the way, it built a family.

In 2026 we took the next step and formally incorporated Ameyali Foundation — a California nonprofit dedicated specifically to supporting children with special needs and disabilities. The team still competes, still rides for nationals, and still supports causes close to us like breast cancer awareness and leukemia. The foundation gives us a structure to do more.

What we do

Three ways we show up.

Our work is just beginning as a foundation, but the team has shown up for the community since 2019. Here's what that looks like now, and what we're building toward.

Time with the horses

We open our ranch to children with special needs and disabilities — to spend time with our horses, meet the team, and experience the calm that being around an animal can bring.

Performances for causes

Each performance is a fundraiser. We've ridden for breast cancer awareness, leukemia, and other community causes — bringing the spectacle of escaramuza to crowds while raising real money.

A structured program (coming)

With foundation status, we're building a more formal program for children with special needs — structured visits, partnerships with families and schools, and the safety standards that come with serving children well.

Performances

Book the team.

Eight riders in full traje charro, choreographed at speed to live or recorded music. Festivals, fundraisers, weddings, community events.

Every booking directly funds the foundation's work with children. The team travels regionally; reach out about your venue and date.

The team

Eight riders. One family.

Daughters, sisters, friends — gathered from the South Bay around a shared love of escaramuza and a stubborn commitment to each other. Off the horse they're students, workers, daughters; on the horse they're a single unit moving as one.

The foundation is led by Evon Munoz (CEO and founder) and Xochil Marquez (CFO and secretary). Individual rider and horse bios will be added as we build this site out.

Photographs from competition, presentations, and quiet moments at the ranch. Tap any image to see it full size.

Get involved

Three ways to support the work.

Donate

One-time or monthly.

Your gift supports children with special needs, the horses that serve them, and the team that rides for both. Recurring gifts make the most difference.

$25 $50 $100 $250
Make a gift
Sponsor

A horse. A child. A performance.

Annual sponsorships cover a specific horse's feed and vet care, a child's program participation, or a performance — with recognition for your business or family.

Become a sponsor
Volunteer

Show up. Lend a hand.

We need volunteers for event days, fundraising, and — as we build our structured program — trained sidewalkers and helpers for children visiting the ranch.
